# technitium_dns_record (Resource)

Manages a DNS record in a Technitium DNS Server authoritative zone.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "technitium_dns_record" "web" {
  zone   = technitium_dns_zone.example.name
  domain = "www.example.com"
  type   = "A"
  value  = "203.0.113.50"
  ttl    = 300
}

resource "technitium_dns_record" "mail" {
  zone     = technitium_dns_zone.example.name
  domain   = "example.com"
  type     = "MX"
  value    = "mail.example.com"
  priority = 10
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `domain` (String) The fully qualified domain name of the record.
- `type` (String) The DNS record type.
- `value` (String) The record value. IP address for A/AAAA, hostname for CNAME/NS/PTR/MX, text for TXT, etc.
- `zone` (String) The authoritative zone name.

### Optional

- `comments` (String) Comments for the record.
- `disabled` (Boolean) Whether the record is disabled.
- `priority` (Number) Priority value for MX and SRV records.
- `ttl` (Number) Time to live in seconds.

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) Composite identifier in the format zone:domain:type:value.
